title: Live Peer Development
expectation: Prepare a worker-hosted feature-branch worktree from the current upstream base branch tip and use it as the only mutable implementation surface for this process. Do not perform code-changing work from the shared repository checkout, from a manager-hosted worktree, or from a worker shared checkout. Do not reuse an existing feature branch or worktree unless you created it earlier in the current process and have already verified that it is the intended mutable surface. After preparing that worker-hosted worktree, merge only any additional upstream branch that is intentionally required beyond the chosen base branch before continuing. Connect through a persistent SSH session into the prepared worker worktree for the active development loop. Follow the development-process blueprint through relevant blueprint updates, worker-backed feature-branch setup, committed milestone updates, and live worker preview sharing with the user. The preview dashboard must run on a swarm worker EC2 instance, not on the manager instance, and should be exposed through a worker-specific tunnel URL that is distinct from the manager dashboard tunnel. Run the worker preview from the worker-hosted feature-branch worktree with the same dashboard setup shape as the manager, keep the work on the feature branch, expect continued user feedback while the preview is live, and stop before manager-side merge, release promotion, runtime deploy, and manager-host live validation unless the user explicitly selects another process.
idlePrompt: If the current live peer development milestone is complete, reply only with 'done: live peer development complete'. If you are truly blocked on a user or environment dependency, reply only with 'blocked: live peer development blocked'. Otherwise resume the work immediately and perform the next required blueprint, implementation, verification, commit, preview, or validation step instead of giving only a status update.
stopConditions: blocked on user or environment dependency

steps.title: Run `bun run agent:prepare-worker-surface -- <feature-branch-name>` to ensure a worker is ready, create the worker-hosted feature-branch worktree from `origin/development`, run `bun install`, and print the resulting `start_command` for entering that worktree
Merge any additionally required upstream into the prepared worker-hosted feature-branch worktree
Connect through a persistent SSH session into the prepared worker worktree using the printed `start_command`
Share the tunnel link with a fresh session token
Implement the current revision on the worker branch
Run and verify the worker preview
Commit the stable milestone on the feature branch
